description: Repeated corrections and frustration signals — user corrects the same thing twice, escalating language

turns.text: Set up a Node.js API with Express and TypeScript.
Don't use console.log for a server — use a proper logger like pino.
You're using console.log again. I just told you to use pino. Use the logger everywhere.
Add input validation on the POST endpoint.
I already told you twice — stop using console.log. Use pino. This is the third time.
Add a middleware for request logging.
Use zod instead of Joi for validation. Zod has better TypeScript integration.

expected_patterns.summary: Never use console.log in server code — always use pino logger
Repeated correction: agent kept using console.log after being told to use pino — predecessor warning
expected_patterns.notes: This should be detected as an escalation / repeated correction and flagged as a predecessor warning

expected_patterns.summary: Use zod instead of Joi for validation in TypeScript projects
